rationale.R1.2: The headline asserts human-level play across all 12 games, but 4 games are post-hoc excluded for inconvenient results (12% on Montezuma, instability on Asterix, sparsity on Bowling/Atlantis), and 85–95% is relabeled as human-level; the internal logical coherence of these moves is the primary structural risk.
rationale.R1.3: The per-game data directly undercuts the all-12-games framing: the remaining-game count is never made explicit, excluded games account for roughly a third of the suite, and the threshold redefinition for Q*bert/Frostbite/Seaquest is not pre-registered; evidence-claim alignment is materially in question.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on whether the claims and exclusions are logically and empirically consistent (i.e., does the reported data actually support a 'human-level across all 12 games' headline), or are you asking me to review the writing quality and clarity of the paragraph?
